claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 17 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period; Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
circumstances: Child is 17 and not in approved education or extension period
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child works 24+ hours/week and is not in approved education
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I'll help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me start the assessment process.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great! To get started, I need to understand your situation. 

**First, can you tell me:**
1. **How many children do you have** (or are asking about)?
2. **What are their names and ages?**

The eligibility for Child Benefit depends on the child's age:
- Children **under 16** typically qualify automatically (with some conditions)
- Children **aged 16-19** may qualify if they're in approved education or training
- Children **aged 20 or over** generally don't qualify

Please share the details about your child or children, and I'll guide you through the eligibility assessment for each one.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text:
Hello! Yes, I'm interested in finding out if I can claim Child Benefit.

I have one child - my son Alex. He's 17 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I can help you determine if you're eligible to claim Child Benefit for your son Alex, who is 17 years old.

Since Alex is 17 (which falls in the 16-19 age range), his eligibility will depend on whether he's in approved education or training.

**Can you tell me: Is Alex currently enrolled in approved education or training?**

This would include:
- Full-time non-advanced education (like A-levels, Scottish Highers, NVQ Level 3 or below)
- Approved vocational training programs
- Education at school or college (not university degree courses)

Is Alex in any of these types of education or training programs?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in any education or training at the moment. He finished school and he's working now - he's got a full-time job at the local supermarket.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information. Let me navigate through the assessment based on Alex's situation.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I see. Since Alex is not currently in education or training, I need to check one more thing:

**When did Alex finish his education?** 

Specifically, has he left education or training within the last 20 weeks? If he recently finished, there may be an extension period during which you could still claim Child Benefit.

Can you tell me approximately when Alex left school or finished his education?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, it's been longer than that. He left school quite a while ago now - it's been several months since he finished. He went straight into work after leaving.

conversation.author: child_benefit_eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
## Child Benefit Eligibility Decision: **Not Eligible**

I'm sorry, but based on the information you've provided, **you are not eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex** at this time.

### Why Alex doesn't qualify:

For young people aged 16-19 (like Alex at 17), Child Benefit can only be claimed if they are:
- In approved full-time non-advanced education or training, **OR**
- Within 20 weeks of leaving education (if they've registered with a careers service or the armed services)

Since Alex:
- Is not currently in education or training
- Left school several months ago (beyond the 20-week extension period)
- Is working full-time

He does not meet the qualifying criteria for Child Benefit.

### What this means:

If you were previously receiving Child Benefit for Alex, it should have stopped when he left education (or up to 20 weeks after, depending on the circumstances).

### If circumstances change:

If Alex decides to return to approved education or training in the future (such as college courses, A-levels, or vocational training), you may become eligible to claim Child Benefit for him again, as long as he's still under 20 years old.

If you have any questions about this decision or need further clarification, please don't hesitate to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 17 years old, which falls within the 16-19 age range. However, he is not currently in approved education or training, and it has been several months since he left school (more than the 20-week extension period). According to the Child Benefit rules, children aged 16-19 only qualify if they are in approved education or training, or within 20 weeks of leaving education (if registered with careers service or armed services). Since Alex left school several months ago and is now working full-time, he does not meet the qualifying criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 17), who is not eligible for Child Benefit because he is not in approved education or training and left school more than 20 weeks ago. He is currently working full-time and therefore does not meet the qualifying child criteria for the 16-19 age group.

response.description: Determine child's age category for eligibility
response.routing_logic: Under 16: automatic qualification. 16-19: check education/training. 20+: not eligible

response.description: For 16-19 year olds, check if in education/training or eligible for extension
response.routing_logic: If in education/training, validate type and level. If just left education (within 20 weeks), check extension eligibility.

response.reason: No qualifying child under 16, or under 20 in approved education/training
response.guidance: Child must be under 16, or aged 16-19 and in approved education or training. If child just turned 20, benefit stops. If 16-17 year old leaves education, benefit can continue for 20 weeks if they register with careers service or armed services.

Since Alex left school several months ago and is now working full-time, he does not meet the qualifying criteria for Child Benefit.

He is currently working full-time and therefore does not meet the qualifying child criteria for the 16-19 age group.
